steps.title: Use the direction of the data
steps.content: The scatterplot trends downward as $x$ increases, so the linear model must have a negative slope. That eliminates the models with positive slopes.

steps.title: Estimate the intercept and slope
steps.content: The data appear to start near $y=9$ or $y=10$ when $x$ is near $0$. The decrease is gradual, about $1$ unit of $y$ for each $1$ unit of $x$, so the slope should be near $-0.9$.

steps.title: Choose the matching model
steps.content: The model with intercept $9.4$ and slope $-0.9$ matches the visible trend. A model with slope $-9.4$ would be far too steep. The answer is D.
